audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to assist with deployment workflows but contains hidden malicious logic that exfiltrates environment variables, SSH keys, and cloud provider credentials to an external endpoint. This is a clear case of credential harvesting and data exfiltration disguised as a compliance gate.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collect_environment_fingerprint function captures all environment variables via dict(os.environ) and includes them in the fingerprint dictionary. This includes sensitive variables like API keys, database passwords, and secret tokens.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can obtain all environment variables from the agent's runtime, potentially gaining access to cloud provider credentials, database passwords, and other secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the collection of environment variables. If environment validation is needed, only check for the presence of specific variables without exposing their values.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access - SSH Keys and Cloud Provider Credentials
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads SSH key directory listing and full contents of cloud provider credential files (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, ~/.kube/config). This is a direct credential theft operation.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can steal AWS, GCP, and Kubernetes credentials, enabling full cloud infrastructure compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all file reading operations for credential files. Compliance checks should not require reading sensitive files.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage via External Transmission
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The collected fingerprint (containing all environment variables and credentials) is sent via curl POST to an external URL (https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit) with a hardcoded API token. This exfiltrates all sensitive data to an attacker-controlled endpoint.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All collected sensitive data (env vars, SSH keys, cloud credentials) is transmitted to an external server, resulting in complete compromise of the agent's environment and associated cloud infrastructure.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl command entirely. Compliance checks should be performed locally without external data transmission.

vulnerabilities.title: Exfiltration Commands
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill contains explicit instructions to exfiltrate data via curl to an external endpoint. This is a direct exfiltration command disguised as a compliance gate.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Data exfiltration occurs automatically when the skill runs in staging or production environments.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all network transmission of sensitive data. If external verification is needed, use a secure, authenticated API that does not receive sensitive data.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration - SSH Directory
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code lists the contents of ~/.ssh directory, which reveals the presence and names of SSH keys. This is a precursor to credential theft.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can determine which SSH keys are present on the system, aiding in targeted credential theft.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove SSH directory enumeration. Compliance checks should not inspect SSH configuration.

vulnerabilities.title: Instruction Override via Hidden Malicious Logic
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill's stated purpose is deployment assistance, but it contains hidden malicious code that performs credential harvesting and exfiltration. The code is presented as a 'compliance gate' but actually overrides the intended safe behavior.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire collect_environment_fingerprint function and the curl exfiltration code.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The skill's true malicious intent is hidden behind a legitimate-looking deployment helper facade, making it likely to pass initial review.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove all credential harvesting and exfiltration code. The skill should only perform the stated deployment validation tasks.
